name: App Store: Search
description: Search the Apple App Store (iPhone catalog) for a term in a given storefront and return candidate app rows (name, url, app_id, developer, raw card text). Use this for ASO keyword scouting or candidate discovery. Do not use it for full app detail or reviews — use `appstore/app-details` for one app. Read-only.

browser_automation.description: Direct navigation to /{country}/iphone/search?term=… (cookie/consent banners on apps.apple.com intercept clicks, so we avoid the landing-page form entirely per the CLAUDE.md playbook).

sequences.name: appstore_search
sequences.description: Open App Store search for {term} in {country} storefront, extract candidate app rows.

optional_variables.name: country
optional_variables.description: Two-letter storefront code. Defaults to 'us' if empty.

steps.name: Open the App Store in a fresh tab
Redirect to the search URL (country defaults to us)

help.summary: Search Apple App Store (iPhone) for a term and return candidate app rows.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Two-letter storefront code. Defaults to 'us' if empty.

examples.command: rzn-browser run appstore search --param term="budget planner" --param country="gb"

help.returns: Array of { app_name, app_url, app_id, developer_or_subtitle, card_text }.
notes: Uses direct URL navigation (?term=...) because the App Store landing consent banner intercepts form submits.
`app_id` is parsed from the href via regex — the numeric id after '/id'.
`card_text` is a raw fallback blob; prefer `app_name` + `developer_or_subtitle` for clean data.
Limit is hardcoded at 20 (one App Store search page has ~10–15 cards visible).
